header.stance: 實質殖利率主導美債賣壓，資金融通有序且信用壓力受控

zh-TW.long_thesis_markdown:
## 固定收益開場主論點

6月3日逆轉前一收盤日溫和的存續期間漲勢，但也確認了當時更重要的政策訊息：美國利率仍在因應名目需求具韌性，以及聯準會可能需要再次緊縮而重新定價。10 年期美債殖利率收在 4.49%，本輪賣壓來自其實質殖利率上升4個基點，而非通膨補償再度增加。這樣的組成顯示，走勢是對實質政策利率路徑的重新評估，而不只是對高昂原油價格的另一次機械式反應。

3條管道推動了這項轉變。民間就業趨於穩定，ISM 服務業指數升至 54.5，商業活動與新訂單均增強。2項服務業調查的就業分項偏軟，使勞動市場展望並非全面穩健，但疲弱程度仍不足以取代市場對需求與價格的關注。WTI 上漲 2.4%，進一步強化這項訊息：美國與伊朗衝突再起，中斷了先前能源壓力的緩解；各地企業聯絡窗口亦表示，燃料成本正擴散至運輸、包裝、食品與肥料。

Fed 溝通將這些發展轉化為更高的實質利率門檻。Lorie Logan 主張，政策可能處於中性、甚至寬鬆狀態，今年稍後或有必要升息。John Williams 認為目前政策設定適當，下一步方向並不明顯，但他的耐心部分建立在能源價格趨穩及2輪效應有限之上。因此，意見分歧在於壓力的持續性與政策限制程度，而非通膨是否仍然過高。

本交易日並非失序的存續期間部位平倉。殖利率曲線僅小幅趨平，國庫券維持有序，4個月期國庫券標售需求穩健，財政部也完成預定的長端流動性買回。信用基金走弱、大盤股票下跌，但半導體股上漲，波動率亦保持受控。即使價格走勢反轉，6月2日的承接主題仍然成立。主導性的解讀是，這是一波由實質殖利率帶動的賣壓：服務業需求較強、油價走堅及偏鷹派政策選擇空間，壓過招聘放緩的證據；同時，公私部門融資有序，避免本輪走勢演變為更廣泛的信用衝擊。

## 美債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構

美債跌勢集中於曲線中段，各期限之間僅有溫和差異。2年期殖利率上升3個基點至 4.08%，5年期上升4個基點至 4.21%，10 年期上升3個基點至 4.49%，30 年期則上升2個基點至 4.99%。2s10s 斜率持平於 41 個基點，2s30s 則趨平1個基點至 91。前端政策風險與中期實質利率壓力同步上升；長債走勢較小，使本交易日未演變為新一波期限溢價衝擊。

整體殖利率曲線仍帶有緊縮風險長期累積的痕跡。過去1個月，2年期與5年期殖利率分別上升 20 與 19 個基點，相較之下，10 年期上升 10 個基點，30 年期上升2個基點。同期 2s10s 與 2s30s 斜率分別收窄 10 與 18 個基點。6月3日延續了這種集中於前端的重新定價，但當日 2s10s 並未出現實質變化，顯示市場提高的是曲線前半段的預期實質政策利率路徑，而非僅將新的財政溢價配置於長端。

曲率顯示，5年期區段位於本輪調整的核心。2s5s10s 蝶式利差上升2個基點至-15，2s10s30s 蝶式利差上升1個基點至-9。兩者仍為負值，但每日變動顯示，繼前一交易日受控的漲勢後，曲線中段相對變便宜。該區段必須同時消化 Fed 可能升息的時點、成長的持久性，以及當前能源壓力可能比即時衝突標題延續更久的前景。

通膨拆解提供了最明確的診斷。5年期 TIPS 殖利率上升3個基點至 1.68%，7年期實質殖利率上升4個基點至 1.89%，10 年期實質殖利率上升4個基點至 2.11%。20 年期與 30 年期實質殖利率均上升3個基點，分別至 2.51% 與 2.72%。10 年期損益兩平通膨率下滑1個基點至 2.38%，完全解釋了名目與實質殖利率變動之間的差距。即使市場通膨補償小幅下滑，投資人仍要求更高的實質補償。

數據組合解釋了這項分歧。ADP 公布 5月民間就業增加 122,000 人，略高於預測中位數，並與前一日高於預期的職缺數據共同顯示就業趨於穩定。S&P Global 服務業指數終值由 51.0 降至 50.7，就業出現 5月 2020 以來最快降幅，企業信心亦偏弱。不過，新業務恢復成長，因此該數據僅削弱勞動市場敘事，尚未證明需求出現廣泛收縮。

ISM 調查提供了更強的利率上行動力。服務業指數由 53.6 升至 54.5，高於 53.7的調查預期。商業活動升至 57.7，新訂單升至 57.3，就業則維持收縮，報 47.9。價格升至 71.3，庫存躍升至追平紀錄的 62.5。就業與價格分項低於部分預期時，美債曾短暫回升，隨後因總指數與需求廣度強化實質利率走高的解讀而再度下跌。

工廠訂單指向相同方向，但強勢表現集中於特定部分。4月訂單增加 4.8%，高於 4.5%的預期，前值則上修為增加 1.8%。出貨增加 1%，未完成訂單增加 1.7%，庫存增加 0.3%。運輸設備貢獻了大部分增幅，激增 21.6% 至 $1,311億。此報告支持生產與資本需求持續，但產業集中度及庫存快速累積，仍使多少動能能轉化為最終需求存在疑問。

儘管損益兩平通膨率小幅下滑，油價仍重新形成外部通膨限制。WTI 上漲 2.4104% 後收於 $96.02，Brent 上漲 1.8854% 至 $97.81。美國與伊朗再度交鋒，降低市場對區域供給快速恢復的信心後，殖利率便已開始上行。隨後褐皮書記錄了更廣泛的成本機制：能源正推升運輸、包裝、食品雜貨及肥料支出；同時，消費者不確定性及支出轉弱跡象則抑制企業展望。

這樣的組合讓 Fed 官員得以各自強調不同面向。Logan 表示，勞動市場大致平衡、金融環境寬鬆，且政策限制性不足以完成抑制通膨的任務。Williams 判斷目前利率適當，並預期服務通膨放緩，但將此評估與中東能源市場最終趨穩連結。尾盤利率交換顯示，年底前升息一碼的機率超過 80%，高於1週前的 60%，且市場已完全納入最晚於 1月 2027 升息的情境。

政策重新定價之下，現貨市場仍維持有序。4週、6週及8週國庫券的票息等值殖利率均下降1個基點，分別至 3.68%、3.69% 及 3.69%。13 週、17 週及 26 週殖利率分別持平於 3.71%、3.74% 及 3.77%，52 週殖利率則上升2個基點至 3.82%。因此，上行調整在1年期及附息美債上較為明顯，而非集中於最短期現金工具。

財政部 $690億的 119 天期國庫券標售，以 3.635%的最高貼現率決標，相當於 3.730%的投資利率，投標倍數為 3.15。最高利率分配比例為 93.35%，該券預定於 6月9日交割。另方面，預定進行的 20 至 30 年期名目附息券流動性買回，在 36 檔合格券中的 14 檔共獲報價 $212.6億，並接受全額 $20億上限。6月4日的交割是較晚發生的現金事件；已完成的操作改善了非指標券流動性，但未改變財政部的淨借款政策。

## 信用、融資與初級市場承接

信用資產在總報酬層面走弱，但並未顯示市場進入管道普遍喪失。LQD 下跌 0.2754%，HYG 下跌 0.2753%，JNK 下跌 0.2183%。三者跌幅接近，反映信用曝險同時受到公債殖利率上升與風險基調轉弱的影響。短期國庫券殖利率穩定、4個月期標售需求穩健，顯示現金融通並非壓力來源。更重要的信用問題是，快速擴張的投資計畫能否持續以有紀律的條件完成融資。

人工智慧融資正由產業主題轉變為市場結構議題。自 2025 年初以來，美國投資級債券、非投資級債券、貸款及其他 AI 相關債務的發行規模已超過 $3,700億。紐約1場信用論壇的市場參與者表示，需求仍然強勁，尤其是由獲利良好的超大規模雲端業者支持債務，或本金可隨時間攤還的交易。他們也警告，若承銷建立在對未來成長的期待上，今日充沛的需求可能演變為明日的槓桿問題。

Alphabet 計畫參與1筆 $10億的加州預付能源市政債交易，說明了創新及承載能力風險。初步文件將該公司列為資金接收方，預期債務評級為 Aa2，用於向 Pioneer Community Energy 提供長期折價電力。該結構讓資金接收方得以享有免稅融資的成本效益，也為投資人提供高於同評級市政債的額外殖利率。這也將使1家美國科技公司成為這個過去由金融機構主導之市場領域的已知參與者。

此結構擴大了可用於資助資料中心電力需求的投資人基礎，但不會創造無限的市政債承載能力。就在超大規模雲端業者的資本需求大幅擴張之際，Alphabet的規模可能替其他同業採用預付能源債券提供背書。由此產生的供給，將使契約保障、交易對手品質，以及1個遠小於全球公司信用市場之市場的深度，更受重視。強大的支持方可能有利於市場進入，但無法消除電力需求集中或依賴單一融資架構的問題。

加拿大初級市場供給提供了更近期的執行檢驗。Coastal GasLink 準備發行 C$10億的2部分投資級債券，其中約 C$3億的到期年限為 11.25 年，另有 C$7億約為 19 年期。初步討論的定價，較短期部分約較公債高 100 個基點，較長期部分則高 115 個基點。加拿大債券到期所產生的預期票息款項可能有助於承接，但最終規模與讓利尚未確定，不能視為需求已獲證實。

信用監測同時變得更具區辨性。聯準會報告指出，2025 年消費、商業及住宅不動產等類別的銀行貸款逾期率溫和上升，但銀行體系的資本仍然充足。數家知名非銀行機構違約，促使銀行重新檢視私募市場曝險的擔保品管理。另外，管理超過 $2,700億的瑞士機構計畫隨持有債券到期，停止購買特定石油生產商的債券；即使高能源價格支撐產業現金流，這仍對特定發行人的買方基礎形成限制。

綜合訊息是，市場仍可進入，但分化更加鮮明。獲得充分支持的 AI 與基礎建設借款人，可進入專門的資本池，短期融資亦保持有序。投資人同時依交易對手、期限、擔保品及投資資格規範進行區別。這使初級市場承接維持完整，但愈來愈依賴交易結構與發行人品質，而非一致性的利差收窄。

## 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導

全球利率傳導出同一種推升美國實質殖利率的成長與通膨衝突。歐元區民間活動連續第2個月收縮：綜合 PMI 終值由 48.8 降至 48.5，服務業則由 47.6 微升至 47.7，仍低於 50。新訂單走弱，就業流失加速。然而，投入與產出價格上漲速度加快，使 ECB 無法單憑成長疲弱便輕易忽略能源衝擊。

生產流程中的通膨進一步強化這項限制。歐元區生產者價格在 4月上漲 0.6%，較1年前上漲 4.9%。不含能源的價格單月上漲 0.9%，中間財上漲 1.8%，即使能源價格下跌 0.4%。細項顯示，壓力已由最初引發政策辯論的波動性成分向外擴散。產出走弱可限制最終的升息週期，但企業成本加速上升，仍使下一步政策與歐洲前端殖利率偏向上行，並推高全球存續期間的均衡利率。

日本則增加了更強的匯率與買方基礎管道。總裁 Kazuo Ueda 表示，若通膨上行風險超過衝突對經濟活動的傷害，6月15日至16日會議就必須考量升息。市場認為升息一碼的機率約為 85%。儘管 4月28日至 5月27日期間進行了創紀錄的 ¥11.73兆干預，日圓仍在每美元 160 附近交易，顯示美日利差與石油進口帳單如何持續對匯率形成壓力。

財政政策在不增加曆年市場供給的情況下，為家庭提供緩衝。日本批准 ¥3.1兆的追加預算，其中包括1筆 ¥2.5兆的準備金，初期用途是協助限制汽油成本。該方案將以赤字融通債券提供資金，但會取消原定發行，使曆年總發行量維持不變。這項差異限制了直接的存續期間負擔，但補貼、日圓疲弱及 BOJ 減少購債，仍使投資人必須評估日本整體通膨應對措施的可信度。

對全球美債的影響不只相對政策利率。BOJ 升息及日本國內殖利率上升，可能降低日本機構投資人對匯率避險後美債的興趣。日圓持續疲弱，也可能使干預及準備管理繼續成為焦點。6月3日這2條管道都未造成美債長端失序，但當政策正常化與能源進口成本同步上升時，兩者都會提高持有全球存續期間所需的補償。

澳洲提供了已開發市場中主要的成長反向力量。第1季 GDP 僅成長 0.3%，為前一季增速的1/3，亦低於預期。民間投資增加近 4%，由資料中心帶動，機械與設備支出上升 16%；家庭支出與淨出口則較弱。3年期公債殖利率回落，因市場轉向預期澳洲央行暫停升息，但單位勞動成本與原油價格偏高，使再次升息仍有可能。

澳洲7年期標售的需求確認，投資人願意在目前殖利率下延伸至曲線中段。對 A$10億、11月 2033 到期債券的投標金額超過發行量4倍，為該期限自前次出售以來最強的投標覆蓋。此結果將成長放緩與具體的存續期間承接結合起來。它並未解決通膨展望，但顯示在澳洲央行正式宣布緊縮週期結束前，經濟放緩風險便可吸引需求。

波蘭顯示，能源壓力不必然導致同步緊縮。央行將基準利率維持在 3.75%，總裁 Adam Glapinski 表示，在 5月通膨率達 3.1%、仍位於目標區間內的情況下，目前設定已足夠高。3×6 個月遠期利率協議較3個月參考利率高 38 個基點，遠低於戰爭爆發後曾反映的 74 個基點升息幅度。儘管進口能源風險依然存在，國內通膨較低仍緩和了全球偏鷹派的衝力。

中國 5月服務業數據提供了最後1股跨境反向力量。服務業指數由 52.6 升至 54.4，綜合指數由 53.1 升至 54.0，需求走強、招聘恢復，投入成本加速上升。銷售價格大致持平，限制了通膨立即向外輸出。中國服務業具韌性及澳洲資料中心投資支撐區域需求，歐元區活動收縮與澳洲債券需求強勁則限制了這項結論。全球存續期間面對成長分化，但政策溢價普遍持續存在。

## 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險

新興市場美元債面對不利的整體環境。EMB 下跌 0.374%，美元指數上漲 0.3124%，原油則變得更昂貴。這種組合提高美國折現率、增加進口能源成本，並收緊缺乏商品收入抵銷之借款人的美元流動性。然而，各國發展仍高度分化：準備累積支撐阿根廷，契約機制使尚比亞的情勢更複雜，而印度的國內融資壓力加劇。

阿根廷央行在週三再買進 $4,300萬後，已超越年度買進 $100億美元的目標。創紀錄的農作物產量、能源出口增加 92%，以及各省與企業的海外借款，均支撐美元流入。達成里程碑降低了市場對近期外幣債務償付的擔憂，並與主權利差收窄及 Fitch 近期升評同時發生。這確實代表美元總取得能力改善，但並未完全解決外部流動性問題。

淨準備仍是限制條件。總準備略高於 $480億，但按市價估算的淨準備接近 $30億，因買進的美元也被用於償債。阿根廷在其 $200億 IMF 計畫的最近一次審查中，未達成淨準備目標，且 2027 年到期付款超過 $300億。強勁出口可以延長付款緩衝期，但重建可用準備仍是檢驗主權韌性的決定性因素。

尚比亞面對的是另一種債務管理障礙。持有其 $13.6億、2053 年到期債券超過 25%的投資人集結成阻擋持股，反對政府買回，從而阻止清理性贖回。該債券價格接近 81 美分，高於 78-美分的要約價，降低了投資人投標交回的誘因。未能償還該債券之所以重要，是因為若 IMF的債務可持續性條件在 6月、並再度於 12月達成，其票息可能由 0.5% 升至 7.5%。決定再融資結果的是談判風險，而非廣泛的新興市場貝塔。

印度顯示，能源衝擊如何從主權債殖利率轉移到銀行資產負債表。自衝突爆發以來，10 年期公債殖利率已上升 38 個基點至 7.04%；企業轉向成本較低的貸款後，國內債券發行量下降 11% 至 10.9兆盧比。截至5月15日止，銀行信貸成長 16.2%，快於存款增速，並推動6個月期存單利率在 5月上升 88 個基點。融資仍然可得，但更多再融資風險已移入短期銀行工具。

迦納通膨率由 3.4% 升至 3.7%，低於 4.3%的預測中位數，但已連續第2次上升。燃料與肥料成本擴散，使食品通膨加速；塞地今年迄今貶值 11%，另增進口價格管道。$140億的總準備可支應 5.5 個月進口，使央行有空間平抑匯率壓力。此結果有利於繼續將政策利率維持在 14%：準備充足可限制即時美元壓力，但未消除控制預期的必要性。

## 跨資產

跨資產價格對實質利率與能源的解讀，強於傳統避險敘事。WTI 上漲 2.4104% 至 $96.02，Brent 上漲 1.8854% 至 $97.81，逆轉先前因市場期待中東供給恢復而出現的部分緩解。此走勢伴隨名目及實質美債殖利率上升，但 10 年期損益兩平通膨率小幅下滑。市場將高昂油價視為維持限制性政策及實質折現率具韌性的理由，而非長期通膨補償再度失去錨定。

股票選擇性走弱。S&P 500 下跌 0.7372%，Nasdaq 100 下跌 0.2914%，但半導體指數上漲 1.3892%。這項分歧符合 AI 資本支出持續、特定產業獲利獲得支撐的經濟情境，而非一致性的衰退交易。VIX 上漲 1.8389% 至 16.06，升幅受控，反映市場有所擔憂，但未顯示廣泛去槓桿。較高的實質殖利率壓抑整體市場，但支撐需求與債務發行的投資主題仍然完整。

美元指數上漲 0.3124% 至 99.53，收緊新興市場借款人的外部環境，並與美國相對利率走堅一致。即使股票下跌，黃金仍下跌 1.1673% 至 $4,436.70，削弱市場普遍湧向地緣政治避險的論點。美元走強、黃金下跌及實質殖利率上升，反而指向金融估值環境收緊。信用基金下跌使這幅圖景更加完整：各類資產的風險基調轉弱，但波動率未飆升，短期國庫券也未失序，使本交易日仍屬有序的政策重新定價。

## 市場前瞻意義

6月4日於 08:30 ET 公布的生產力與單位勞動成本修正值及初領失業救濟金人數，接著是 6月5日於 08:30 ET 公布的就業報告，以及 6月10日的 5月 CPI，將檢驗 6月3日實質殖利率上升究竟反映持久需求，還是市場對好壞參半之調查數據反應過度。可觀察的觸發因素包括：生產力初值成長 0.8% 與單位勞動成本 2.3%的修正、相對前值 215,000的初領失業救濟金人數、相對保留預期 93,000的非農就業成長、相對 4.3%的失業率，以及整體、核心與住宅通膨的廣度。招聘、勞動成本與服務通膨若保持穩健，將確認實質利率走高的解讀，並使2年期、5年期與 10 年期殖利率持續面臨 Fed 提前升息的風險。若勞動市場明顯降溫，且基礎價格漲幅放慢，則會挑戰這項解讀，重新開啟政策辯論中的寬鬆方向，並化解 ISM 需求強勁與服務業就業指標收縮之間的矛盾。

中東能源流動與下一波全球政策決策，將檢驗這波衝擊正演變為同步緊縮週期，或仍只是各國不同的應對。政策序列包括 6月5日的印度央行決策、6月10日的加拿大央行決策、下一週的 ECB 會議、6月15日至16日的 BOJ 會議，以及 6月16日至17日的 Fed 會議。可觀察的觸發因素包括原油的持續變化、荷莫茲海峽航運恢復情況、能源進口經濟體的貨幣表現，以及官員究竟強調暫時性的整體通膨影響，還是更廣泛的薪資、服務與預期壓力。供給安全、油價下跌及耐心的政策指引，將挑戰當前全球前端溢價，並緩解損益兩平通膨率、日本買方基礎風險及新興市場融資壓力。若干擾持續、貨幣走弱或各央行協調釋出偏鷹派指引，則會確認能源至政策的傳導，並使實質殖利率與跨境折現率維持高檔。

美國財政部與公司債供給將檢驗 6月3日的有序承接能否撐過更大規模的執行。6月4日將正式公告下一週的3年期、10 年期與 30 年期附息債供給，以及 13 週與 26 週國庫券；另將於 13:40 至 14:00 ET 進行最高 $125億、標的為1個月至2年期附息債的現金管理買回，於 6月5日交割。3年期標售接著於 6月9日舉行，10 年期增額發行及另一場 17 週國庫券標售則在 6月10日進行；Alphabet的預付能源融資與 Coastal GasLink的2部分發行，也構成民間部門的檢驗。可觀察的觸發因素包括公告規模與條款、標售決標殖利率、投標覆蓋與分配、國庫券利率穩定、交割順暢，以及公司債各券次能否維持計畫規模而不需大幅擴大讓利。結果若保持有序，將確認推動賣壓的是總體重新定價，而非資產負債表稀缺。需求疲弱或交割壓力則會挑戰這項結論，使供給與融資重新成為殖利率曲線及信用分化的驅動因素。
zh-TW.methodology_note: 除非另有註明，市場水準均以紐約收盤為準。

overall_summary.headline: 需求具韌性、油價走堅及 Fed 政策選擇空間壓過招聘疲軟，推升實質殖利率
overall_summary.session_narrative: 紐約收盤時，美債殖利率上升，曲線中段與實質殖利率消化了較強的 ISM 服務業需求、再起的油價壓力，以及 Fed 可能再度升息的情境。損益兩平通膨率小幅下滑，顯示市場面對的是更高的實質利率門檻，而非通膨預期失去錨定。招聘訊號好壞參半、國庫券穩定、標售需求穩健且波動率受控，使賣壓維持有序，未演變為更廣泛的融資或信用衝擊。
overall_summary.summary: 紐約收盤時，前一日的存續期間漲勢反轉，因服務業需求轉強、油價上漲及再度升息的風險，提高了市場對實質政策利率路徑的預期。招聘訊號偏軟，加上現金融通與信用融資有序，限制了走勢幅度，使本次變動維持為有序的重新定價，而非廣泛的信用衝擊。

section_summaries.market_read: 本輪賣壓反映更高的實質政策利率門檻，而非失序的存續期間或融資壓力。
section_summaries.next_check: 招聘走軟能否推翻需求具韌性的判斷，是檢驗本輪實質殖利率走勢最明確的關鍵。
section_summaries.summary: 服務業需求穩健、油價上漲及 Fed 偏鷹派的政策選擇空間推升實質殖利率，美債因而逆轉前一日漲勢。較疲軟的招聘指標削弱了訊號強度，而穩定的國庫券、穩健的標售需求及受控的波動率則顯示，重新定價仍屬有序，未演變成廣泛的信用衝擊。

section_summaries.market_read: 曲線中段仍是 Fed 緊縮時點提前最清楚的表達，而非新的財政溢價。
section_summaries.next_check: 勞動成本與非農就業廣度必須進一步確認，市場預期的實質政策利率路徑是否會維持在較高水準。
section_summaries.section_title: 美債利率、Fed 定價、實質殖利率與殖利率曲線結構
section_summaries.summary: 曲線中段殖利率領漲，帶動前端集中的重新定價；實質殖利率上升、損益兩平通膨率下滑，長端則相對受控。強勁的 ISM 需求與再起的油價壓力，壓過服務業就業訊號好壞參半的影響；國庫券與流動性操作有序，顯示推動殖利率曲線的是政策預期，而非現金短缺。

section_summaries.market_read: 信用市場仍然開放，但選擇性提高，交易結構與發行人品質的重要性上升。
section_summaries.next_check: 即將進行的公司債定價與最終讓利，將顯示有紀律的承接能否延續。
section_summaries.section_title: 信用、融資與初級市場承接
section_summaries.summary: 信用基金隨公債殖利率上升而走弱，但現金融通穩定且初級市場仍可進入，不支持壓力已全面擴散的判斷。AI 與基礎建設融資拓寬了發行管道，但承接能力愈來愈取決於交易對手實力、契約保障、期限及讓利，而非一致性的需求。

section_summaries.market_read: 儘管各地區成長結果分歧，美國存續期間如今仍承載全球政策溢價。
section_summaries.next_check: 油價、日圓及央行指引將顯示緊縮是否趨於同步。
section_summaries.section_title: 全球存續期間、央行與財政傳導
section_summaries.summary: 全球存續期間面對成長分化，但政策溢價持續存在；歐洲成本、日本政策正常化風險及能源壓力推高了跨境折現率。澳洲成長偏軟、債券需求強勁及波蘭政策持穩，緩和了這股力量，而日本的買方基礎與匯率管道仍是關鍵限制。

section_summaries.market_read: 決定新興市場風險的因素，更多是可用流動性與債務機制，而非廣泛的市場貝塔。
section_summaries.next_check: 阿根廷的淨準備與尚比亞的買回條款，是檢驗主權韌性最明確的檢驗指標。
section_summaries.section_title: 新興市場主權債、美元流動性與債務管理風險
section_summaries.summary: 新興市場美元債消化了美元走強、美國折現率上升及能源成本增加的影響，但各主權債表現分歧。阿根廷取得美元的總額改善，但可用準備仍然不足；尚比亞面臨債券持有人阻擋的風險，印度將再融資轉向銀行，而迦納則保有準備支撐的政策空間。

section_summaries.market_read: 風險資產消化了實質殖利率上升，未出現波動率飆升或一致性的衰退訊號。
section_summaries.next_check: 油價與美元若持續走強，將考驗有序的重新定價是否會發展成更廣泛的壓力。
section_summaries.summary: 跨資產走勢確認這是1場有序的實質利率衝擊：油價與美元上漲，黃金與大盤股票下跌，半導體則持續走強。信用基金走軟，波動率僅溫和上升，顯示估值環境收緊，但未出現全面去槓桿或湧向避險資產的典型特徵。

section_summaries.market_read: 下一步走勢取決於總體面的持續性或資產負債表稀缺，何者能驗證本輪賣壓。
section_summaries.next_check: 勞動數據、全球政策會議及標售執行情況，是具有決定性的可觀察檢驗。
section_summaries.summary: 3項檢驗將決定收盤時「實質殖利率上升且承接有序」的解讀能否成立：美國勞動與通膨數據、能源流動與協調一致的政策指引，以及更大的公私部門供給。需求穩健、干擾持續或標售疲弱，都將強化利率趨緊的判斷；數據降溫或執行順暢則會對其構成挑戰。

watch_events.market_implication: 若獲確認，2年期、5年期及 10 年期殖利率將持續面臨 Fed 提前升息的風險。
watch_events.market_trigger: 招聘、勞動成本與服務通膨若保持穩健，將驗證需求訊號具有持久性。
watch_events.title: 美國勞動、生產力與通膨檢驗
watch_events.watch_line: 追蹤生產力與勞動成本修正值、初領失業救濟金人數、非農就業廣度及基礎通膨。

watch_events.market_implication: 實質殖利率、全球前端利率及新興市場融資壓力將維持高檔。
watch_events.market_trigger: 干擾持續、貨幣走弱或各央行協調釋出偏鷹派指引，都將延長這波衝擊。
watch_events.title: 能源流動與全球政策序列
watch_events.watch_line: 監測原油、荷莫茲海峽航運、能源進口國貨幣及央行指引。

watch_events.market_implication: 供給與融資壓力將重新成為殖利率曲線及信用分化的驅動因素。
watch_events.market_trigger: 標售需求疲弱或公司債讓利明顯擴大，將挑戰承接有序的判斷。
watch_events.title: 美國財政部與公司債供給承接
watch_events.watch_line: 關注公告規模、標售結果、國庫券穩定性、交割及公司債讓利。

long_thesis.english_markdown:
## Opening Fixed-Income Thesis

June 3 reversed the prior close’s modest duration rally but confirmed its more important policy message: US rates were still being repriced for resilient nominal demand and a Federal Reserve that may need to tighten again. The 10-year Treasury yield ended at 4.49%, and the selloff came through a 4-basis-point rise in its real yield rather than a renewed increase in inflation compensation. That composition made the move a reassessment of the real policy path, not simply another mechanical response to expensive crude.

3 channels drove the shift. Private payrolls stabilized, and the ISM services index reached 54.5 as business activity and new orders strengthened. Softer employment components in two services surveys kept the labor outlook from looking uniformly firm, yet they were not weak enough to displace the market’s focus on demand and prices. WTI’s 2.4% advance compounded that message: renewed US-Iran conflict interrupted the earlier energy relief, and regional business contacts described fuel costs spreading into shipping, packaging, food and fertilizer.

Fed communication converted those developments into a higher real-rate hurdle. Lorie Logan argued that policy may be neutral or even accommodative and could require an increase later this year. John Williams saw current settings as appropriate and no obvious next direction, but his patience rested partly on energy stabilization and limited second-round effects. The disagreement was therefore about persistence and the degree of restraint, not about whether inflation remained too high.

The session was not a disorderly duration liquidation. The curve flattened only slightly, bills remained orderly, a 4-month bill auction drew solid demand, and Treasury completed its scheduled long-end liquidity buyback. Credit funds weakened and broad equities fell, but semiconductor shares rose and volatility stayed contained. June 2’s absorption theme survived even as its price action reversed. The governing interpretation is a real-yield-led selloff in which stronger services demand, firmer oil and hawkish policy optionality outweighed evidence of slower hiring, while orderly public and private funding prevented the move from becoming a broader credit shock.

## UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure

Treasury losses were concentrated in the belly, with only modest differentiation across maturities. The 2-year yield rose 3 basis points to 4.08%, the 5-year climbed 4 to 4.21%, the 10-year added 3 to 4.49%, and the 30-year increased 2 to 4.99%. The 2s10s slope was unchanged at 41 basis points, while 2s30s flattened 1 basis point to 91. Front-end policy risk and intermediate real-rate pressure rose together; the smaller long-bond move kept the session from becoming a fresh term-premium shock.

The broader curve still carries the cumulative imprint of tightening risk. Over 1 month, 2- and 5-year yields were higher by 20 and 19 basis points, compared with increases of 10 at 10 years and two at 30 years. The 2s10s and 2s30s slopes narrowed by 10 and 18 basis points over that horizon. June 3 extended that front-loaded repricing without materially changing 2s10s on the day, showing that the market raised the expected real-policy path across the front half rather than assigning a new fiscal premium exclusively to the long end.

Curvature placed the 5-year sector at the center of the adjustment. The 2s5s10s butterfly rose 2 basis points to -15, and the 2s10s30s butterfly increased 1 basis point to -9. Both remained negative, but their daily moves marked relative cheapening in the belly after the previous session’s controlled rally. That sector must reconcile the timing of a possible Fed increase with the durability of growth and the prospect that current energy pressure outlasts the immediate conflict headlines.

Inflation decomposition supplied the clearest diagnosis. The 5-year TIPS yield rose 3 basis points to 1.68%, 7-year real yields climbed 4 to 1.89%, and the 10-year real yield increased 4 to 2.11%. Real yields at 20 and 30 years each rose 3 basis points, to 2.51% and 2.72%. The 10-year breakeven slipped 1 basis point to 2.38%, fully accounting for the gap between the nominal and real moves. Investors demanded more real compensation even as market inflation compensation eased marginally.

The data mix explains that divergence. ADP reported a 122,000 increase in May private employment, slightly above the median forecast and consistent with stabilization after the preceding day’s stronger-than-expected openings data. The final S&P Global services index eased to 50.7 from 51.0, with the fastest employment decline since May 2020 and weak business confidence. Its new business nevertheless returned to growth, so it qualified the labor story without establishing a broad contraction in demand.

ISM’s survey provided the stronger rate impulse. Its services index rose to 54.5 from 53.6, above the 53.7 survey expectation. Business activity increased to 57.7 and new orders to 57.3, while employment remained in contraction at 47.9. Prices rose to 71.3 and inventories jumped to a record-tying 62.5. Treasuries briefly recovered when the employment and price components came in below some expectations, then sold off as the headline and demand breadth reinforced a higher-real-rate interpretation.

Factory orders pointed in the same direction, though with a concentrated composition. April orders increased 4.8%, above the 4.5% expectation and following a revised 1.8% gain. Shipments rose 1%, unfilled orders 1.7%, and inventories 0.3%. Transportation equipment accounted for much of the strength, surging 21.6% to $131.1 billion. The release supported continued production and capital demand, but the sector concentration and rapid inventory accumulation leave open how much momentum will translate into final demand.

Oil restored the external inflation constraint even though breakevens edged lower. WTI settled at $96.02 after a 2.4104% gain, and Brent rose 1.8854% to $97.81. Yields were already moving higher after renewed US-Iran exchanges reduced confidence in a quick restoration of regional supply. The Beige Book then documented a wider cost mechanism: energy was raising expenses for transport, packaging, groceries and fertilizer, even as consumer uncertainty and signs of weaker spending restrained business outlooks.

That combination gave Fed officials room for different emphases. Logan said the labor market was broadly balanced, financial conditions accommodative and policy insufficiently restrictive to finish the inflation task. Williams judged the current rate appropriate and expected services inflation to slow, but linked that assessment to eventual stabilization in Middle East energy markets. Late-session swaps implied more than an 80% chance of a quarter-point increase by year-end, up from 60% a week earlier, with a move fully embedded by January 2027.

Cash markets remained orderly beneath the policy repricing. 4-, 6- and 8-week coupon-equivalent bill yields each declined 1 basis point, to 3.68%, 3.69% and 3.69%. The 13-, 17- and 26-week yields were unchanged at 3.71%, 3.74% and 3.77%, while the 52-week yield rose 2 basis points to 3.82%. The upward adjustment was therefore more visible at 1 year and in coupon Treasuries than in the shortest cash instruments.

Treasury’s $69 billion 119-day bill auction cleared at a 3.635% high discount rate, equivalent to a 3.730% investment rate, with a 3.15 bid-to-cover ratio. Allotment at the high was 93.35%, and the issue is due to settle June 9. Separately, the scheduled 20- to 30-year nominal-coupon liquidity buyback accepted the full $2 billion maximum from $21.26 billion offered across 14 of 36 eligible securities. Its June 4 settlement is a later cash event; the completed operation improved off-the-run liquidity without changing Treasury’s net borrowing policy.

## Credit, Funding, And Primary-Market Absorption

Credit weakened in total-return terms without showing a generalized loss of market access. LQD fell 0.2754%, HYG declined 0.2753%, and JNK lost 0.2183%. Their tightly grouped moves reflected higher government yields and a softer risk tone alongside credit exposure. Stable short bill yields and solid 4-month auction demand indicated that cash funding was not the source of the pressure. The more important credit question was whether a rapidly expanding investment pipeline could keep clearing at disciplined terms.

Artificial-intelligence financing is moving from a sector theme toward a market-structure issue. More than $370 billion of US high-grade bonds, junk bonds, loans and other AI-linked debt has been issued since the start of 2025. Market participants at a New York credit forum said demand remained strong, particularly where profitable hyperscalers support the obligation or principal amortizes over time. They also warned that underwriting based on hoped-for future growth could turn today’s abundant demand into tomorrow’s leverage problem.

Alphabet’s planned role in a $1 billion California prepaid-energy municipal transaction illustrates both innovation and capacity risk. Preliminary documents name the company as funding recipient for debt expected to carry an Aa2 rating and finance long-term discounted electricity for Pioneer Community Energy. The structure gives the recipient access to tax-exempt funding economics and offers investors additional yield over similarly rated municipal debt. It would also make a US technology company a known participant in a segment previously dominated by financial firms.

The structure broadens the investor base available to finance data-center electricity needs, but it does not create unlimited municipal capacity. Alphabet’s scale could validate prepaid energy bonds for other hyperscalers just as their capital requirements expand sharply. The resulting supply will place more weight on contractual protections, counterparty quality and the depth of a market much smaller than global corporate credit. Strong sponsorship may support access; it does not eliminate concentration in power demand or a single funding architecture.

Canadian primary supply provided a nearer execution test. Coastal GasLink prepared a C$1 billion 2-part high-grade sale, comprising roughly C$300 million with an 11.25-year maturity and C$700 million at about 19 years. Initial discussion was near 100 basis points over government bonds for the shorter piece and 115 basis points for the longer one. Expected coupon payments from maturing Canadian bonds could aid absorption, but final sizing and concessions remained unresolved rather than completed evidence of demand.

Credit surveillance became more discriminating at the same time. The Federal Reserve reported a modest increase in 2025 bank-loan delinquencies across consumer, commercial and residential real estate categories, though the system remained well capitalized. Several prominent nonbank defaults have prompted banks to revisit collateral management for private-market exposures. Separately, Swiss institutions managing more than $270 billion plan to stop buying bonds from specified oil producers as holdings mature, creating an issuer-specific buyer-base constraint even as high energy prices support sector cash flow.

The combined message is access with sharper segmentation. Well-supported AI and infrastructure borrowers can reach specialized pools of capital, and short-term funding remains orderly. Investors are simultaneously differentiating by counterparty, maturity, collateral and eligibility mandate. That leaves primary absorption intact, but increasingly dependent on deal structure and issuer quality rather than on uniform spread compression.

## Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission

Global rates transmitted the same growth-inflation conflict that lifted US real yields. Euro-area private activity contracted for a 2nd month: the final composite PMI fell to 48.5 from 48.8, while services edged to 47.7 from 47.6 and remained below 50. New orders weakened and employment losses accelerated. Yet input and output prices rose faster, denying the ECB a clean growth-based reason to look through the energy shock.

Pipeline inflation reinforced that constraint. Euro-area producer prices rose 0.6% in April and 4.9% from a year earlier. Prices excluding energy increased 0.9% on the month, and intermediate goods rose 1.8%, even as energy prices declined 0.4%. The details show pressure spreading beyond the volatile component that initiated the policy debate. Weaker output can cap the eventual tightening cycle, but faster business costs keep the next policy move and European front-end yields pointed upward, raising the global clearing rate for duration.

Japan added a stronger currency and buyer-base channel. Governor Kazuo Ueda said the June 15-16 meeting must weigh an increase if upside inflation risk exceeds the conflict’s damage to activity. Markets assigned roughly an 85% chance to a quarter-point move. The yen traded near 160 per dollar despite a record ¥11.73 trillion of intervention between April 28 and May 27, showing how the US-Japan rate gap and the oil import bill continued to work against the currency.

Fiscal policy cushioned households without adding calendar-year market supply. Japan approved a ¥3.1 trillion supplementary budget, including a ¥2.5 trillion reserve intended initially to help cap gasoline costs. Deficit-financing bonds will fund the package, but previously scheduled issuance will be canceled so total calendar issuance remains unchanged. That distinction limits the direct duration burden, even as subsidies, a weak yen and reduced BOJ purchases leave investors to assess the credibility of Japan’s broader inflation response.

The global Treasury implication extends beyond relative policy rates. A BOJ increase and higher domestic yields can reduce the attraction of currency-hedged US bonds for Japanese institutions. Persistent yen weakness may also keep intervention and reserve management in focus. Neither channel produced a disorderly US long end on June 3, but both raise the compensation needed to hold global duration when policy normalization and energy-import costs are moving together.

Australia supplied the main developed-market growth counterweight. Q1 GDP rose only 0.3%, 1/3 of the previous quarter’s pace and below expectations. Private investment climbed almost 4%, led by data centers, with machinery and equipment spending up 16%; household spending and net exports were weaker. 3-year government yields retreated as markets leaned toward an RBA pause, though elevated unit labor costs and crude prices kept another increase possible.

Demand at the Australian 7-year auction confirmed that investors were willing to extend into the belly at current yields. Bids exceeded 4 times the A$1 billion of November 2033 notes offered, the strongest cover for that maturity since its previous sale. The result paired softer growth with concrete duration absorption. It did not settle the inflation outlook, but it showed that slowdown risk can attract demand before the RBA formally declares the tightening cycle complete.

Poland demonstrated that energy pressure need not generate synchronized tightening. The central bank kept its benchmark at 3.75%, and Governor Adam Glapinski said the setting was sufficiently high after May inflation reached 3.1%, still inside the target band. Three-by-6-month forward-rate agreements traded 38 basis points above the 3-month reference rate, well below the 74 basis points of tightening once priced after the war began. Softer domestic inflation moderated the global hawkish impulse even though imported-energy risk remained.

China’s May services data offered a final cross-border counterforce. The services index rose to 54.4 from 52.6 and the composite to 54.0 from 53.1, with stronger demand, renewed hiring and faster input costs. Selling prices were broadly unchanged, limiting the immediate export of inflation. Resilient Chinese services and Australian data-center investment support regional demand, while contracting euro-area activity and strong Australian bond demand restrain the conclusion. Global duration faced uneven growth but a broadly persistent policy premium.

## EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk

Emerging-market dollar debt faced an adverse broad configuration. EMB fell 0.374%, the dollar index rose 0.3124%, and crude became more expensive. That mix raises the US discount rate, increases imported-energy costs and tightens dollar liquidity for borrowers without offsetting commodity revenue. Country developments nevertheless remained highly differentiated, with reserve accumulation supporting Argentina as contractual mechanics complicated Zambia and domestic funding pressure intensified in India.

Argentina passed its $10 billion annual dollar-purchase goal after the central bank acquired another $43 million on Wednesday. Record crop volumes, a 92% increase in energy exports and foreign borrowing by provinces and companies supported the inflow. The milestone reduces immediate concern over hard-currency debt service and coincided with narrower sovereign spreads and a recent Fitch upgrade. It is a genuine improvement in gross acquisition capacity, not a complete resolution of external liquidity.

Net reserves remain the limiting condition. Total reserves stood just above $48 billion, but net reserves at market prices were estimated near $3 billion because purchased dollars have also been used to service debt. Argentina missed the net-reserve target in the latest review of its $20 billion IMF program, and more than $30 billion of payments fall due in 2027. Strong exports can extend the payment runway; rebuilding usable reserves is still the decisive test of sovereign resilience.

Zambia faced a debt-management obstacle of a different kind. Holders controlling more than 25% of its $1.36 billion 2053 bond assembled a blocking stake against the government’s buyback, preventing a clean-up call. The notes traded near 81 cents, above the 78-cent offer, reducing the incentive to tender. Failure to retire the bond matters because its coupon may rise from 0.5% to 7.5% if an IMF debt-sustainability condition is met in June and again in December. Negotiation risk, not broad EM beta, determines the refinancing outcome.

India showed how the energy shock can migrate from sovereign yields into bank balance sheets. The 10-year government yield had risen 38 basis points to 7.04% since the conflict began, and domestic bond issuance fell 11% to 10.9 trillion rupees as companies shifted toward cheaper loans. Bank credit expanded 16.2% in the year through May 15, outpacing deposit growth and pushing 6-month certificate-of-deposit rates up 88 basis points in May. Funding remained available, but more refinancing risk moved into short-term bank instruments.

Ghana’s inflation rose to 3.7% from 3.4%, below the 4.3% median forecast but the 2nd consecutive increase. Food inflation accelerated as fuel and fertilizer costs spread, while an 11% year-to-date cedi decline added an imported-price channel. Gross reserves of $14 billion covered 5.5 months of imports, giving the central bank room to smooth currency pressure. The result favors a continued 14% policy hold: reserve adequacy limits immediate dollar stress, yet it does not remove the need to contain expectations.

## Cross asset

Cross-asset prices reinforced the real-rate and energy interpretation more than a conventional haven narrative. WTI rose 2.4104% to $96.02 and Brent gained 1.8854% to $97.81, reversing part of the earlier relief tied to hopes for restored Middle East supply. The move coincided with higher nominal and real Treasury yields, yet the 10-year breakeven edged lower. Markets treated expensive oil as a reason for restrictive policy and resilient real discount rates rather than as a fresh unanchoring of long-run inflation compensation.

Equities weakened selectively. The S&P 500 fell 0.7372% and the Nasdaq 100 lost 0.2914%, but the semiconductor index gained 1.3892%. The divergence fits an economy with continued AI capital spending and sector-specific earnings support, not a uniform recession trade. The VIX rose 1.8389% to 16.06, a contained increase that registered concern without signaling broad deleveraging. Higher real yields weighed on the overall market while the investment theme that supports demand and debt issuance remained intact.

The dollar index gained 0.3124% to 99.53, tightening the external backdrop for EM borrowers and aligning with firmer relative US rates. Gold fell 1.1673% to $4,436.70 even as equities declined, weakening the case for a generalized rush into geopolitical protection. A stronger dollar, lower gold and higher real yields point instead to tighter financial valuation conditions. Credit-fund declines completed that picture: risk softened across assets, but the absence of a volatility spike or short-bill dislocation kept the session inside an orderly policy repricing.

## Forward Market Significance

The June 4 revised productivity and unit-labor-cost release and initial claims at 08:30 ET, followed by the June 5 employment report at 08:30 ET and May CPI on June 10, will test whether June 3’s real-yield rise reflects durable demand or an overreaction to mixed surveys. The observable triggers are revisions to preliminary productivity growth of 0.8% and unit labor costs of 2.3%, claims against the prior 215,000 reading, payroll growth around the retained 93,000 expectation, unemployment relative to 4.3%, and the breadth of headline, core and shelter inflation. Firm hiring, labor costs and services inflation would confirm the higher-real-rate interpretation and keep 2-, 5- and 10-year yields exposed to an earlier Fed increase. Clear labor cooling paired with slower underlying prices would challenge it, reopen the easing side of the policy debate and resolve the tension between strong ISM demand and contracting services-employment measures.

Middle East energy flows and the next global policy decisions will test whether the shock is becoming a synchronized tightening cycle or remains a set of country-specific responses. The RBI decision on June 5, the Bank of Canada decision on June 10, the ECB meeting the following week, the BOJ meeting on June 15-16 and the Fed meeting on June 16-17 provide the sequence. Observable triggers are sustained changes in crude, restored Strait of Hormuz traffic, currency performance in energy-importing economies, and whether officials emphasize temporary headline effects or broader wage, services and expectations pressure. Secure supply, lower oil and patient guidance would challenge the current global front-end premium and relieve breakevens, Japanese buyer-base risk and EM funding pressure. Continued disruption, currency weakness or coordinated hawkish guidance would confirm the energy-policy transmission and keep real yields and cross-border discount rates elevated.

Treasury and corporate supply will test whether June 3’s orderly absorption survives heavier execution. June 4 brings formal announcements for the following week’s 3-, 10- and 30-year coupon supply and 13- and 26-week bills, plus a cash-management buyback of up to $12.5 billion in 1-month to 2-year coupons from 13:40 to 14:00 ET for June 5 settlement. The 3-year auction follows June 9, with the 10-year reopening and another 17-week bill sale on June 10; Alphabet’s prepaid-energy financing and Coastal GasLink’s 2-part sale add private-sector tests. Observable triggers are announced sizes and terms, auction clearing yields, cover and allocations, stable bill rates, smooth settlements, and whether corporate tranches retain planned size without materially wider concessions. Orderly results would confirm that macro repricing, not balance-sheet scarcity, drove the selloff. Weak demand or settlement pressure would challenge that conclusion and reopen supply and funding as drivers of the curve and credit differentiation.

section_summaries.market_read: The selloff reflects a higher real-policy hurdle, not disorderly duration or funding stress.
section_summaries.next_check: Whether softer hiring can overturn resilient demand is the clearest test of the real-yield move.
section_summaries.summary: Treasuries reversed the prior rally as firm services demand, rising oil and hawkish Fed optionality lifted real yields. Softer hiring measures qualified the signal, while stable bills, solid auction demand and contained volatility showed that the repricing remained orderly rather than becoming a broad credit shock.

section_summaries.market_read: The belly remains the clearest expression of tighter Fed timing rather than a new fiscal premium.
section_summaries.next_check: Labor costs and payroll breadth must confirm whether the higher expected real-policy path persists.
section_summaries.section_title: UST Rates, Fed Pricing, Real Yields, And Curve Structure
section_summaries.summary: Belly yields led a front-loaded repricing as real yields rose and breakevens slipped, leaving the long end comparatively contained. Strong ISM demand and renewed oil pressure outweighed mixed services employment, while orderly bills and liquidity operations showed policy expectations, not cash scarcity, drove the curve.

section_summaries.market_read: Credit remains open but more selective, with structure and issuer quality carrying greater weight.
section_summaries.next_check: Upcoming corporate pricing and final concessions will show whether disciplined absorption survives.
section_summaries.summary: Credit funds weakened with higher government yields, but stable cash funding and primary-market access argued against generalized stress. AI and infrastructure financing broadened issuance channels, yet absorption increasingly depended on counterparty strength, contractual protection, maturity and concessions rather than uniform demand.

section_summaries.market_read: US duration now carries a global policy premium despite diverging regional growth outcomes.
section_summaries.next_check: Oil, the yen and central-bank guidance will reveal whether tightening becomes synchronized.
section_summaries.section_title: Global Duration, Central Banks, And Fiscal Transmission
section_summaries.summary: Global duration faced uneven growth but a persistent policy premium as European costs, Japanese normalization risk and energy pressure lifted cross-border discount rates. Softer Australian growth, strong bond demand and Poland's steady policy tempered the impulse, while Japan's buyer-base and currency channels remained key constraints.

section_summaries.market_read: EM risk is being set by usable liquidity and debt mechanics more than by broad market beta.
section_summaries.next_check: Argentina's net reserves and Zambia's buyback terms are the clearest sovereign resilience tests.
section_summaries.section_title: EM Sovereigns, Dollar Liquidity, And Debt-Management Risk
section_summaries.summary: EM dollar debt absorbed a stronger dollar, higher US discount rates and costlier energy, but sovereign outcomes diverged. Argentina improved gross dollar acquisition while usable reserves stayed thin; Zambia faced bondholder blocking risk, India shifted refinancing toward banks, and Ghana retained reserve-supported policy space.

section_summaries.market_read: Risk assets absorbed higher real yields without a volatility spike or uniform recession signal.
section_summaries.next_check: Sustained oil and dollar strength would test whether orderly repricing develops broader stress.
section_summaries.summary: Cross assets confirmed an orderly real-rate shock: oil and the dollar rose, gold and broad equities fell, and semiconductor strength persisted. Credit funds softened and volatility increased only modestly, indicating tighter valuation conditions without the hallmarks of generalized deleveraging or a haven rush.

section_summaries.market_read: The next move hinges on whether macro persistence or balance-sheet scarcity validates the selloff.
section_summaries.next_check: Labor data, global policy meetings and auction execution are the decisive observable checks.
section_summaries.summary: 3 tests will determine whether the close's real-yield and orderly-absorption interpretation holds: US labor and inflation data, energy flows and coordinated policy guidance, and heavier public and private supply. Firm demand, persistent disruption or weak auctions would reinforce tighter rates; cooling or smooth execution would challenge it.

methodology_note: Market levels are shown on an NY-close basis unless otherwise noted.

UST 2s10s curve 41 bp (0 bp) | UST 2s30s curve 91 bp (-1 bp)
overall_summary.headline: Real yields rise as resilient demand, firmer oil and Fed optionality outweigh soft hiring
overall_summary.session_narrative: At the NY close, Treasury yields were higher as the belly and real yields absorbed stronger ISM services demand, renewed oil pressure and the possibility of another Fed increase. Breakevens edged lower, identifying a higher real-rate hurdle rather than unanchored inflation expectations. Mixed hiring signals, stable bills, solid auction demand and contained volatility kept the selloff orderly and stopped it from becoming a broader funding or credit shock.
overall_summary.summary: The NY close reversed the prior duration rally as stronger services demand, higher oil and renewed tightening risk raised the expected real-policy path. Softer hiring signals and orderly cash and credit funding limited the move, leaving an orderly repricing rather than a broad credit shock.
